story.iWant: to retry my prompts with modifications
story.soThat: I can refine responses without losing conversation context
story.tasks: - Add Retry button to user messages - Implement retry modal/input pre-filled with original prompt - Handle attachment management (carry forward, add/remove) - Implement createRetry() in ThreadService with branch limit validation - Handle errors gracefully (branch limit, API failures)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Retry button visible on user messages only (not assistant) (TM §3.1)
Clicking Retry opens input with original prompt pre-filled (TM §3.1)
Original attachments shown as editable chips (ARCH §3.2)
User can remove/add attachments before submitting (ARCH §3.2)
Kept attachments use shared fileId references, not copies (ARCH §3.2)
User can edit prompt text before submitting (TM §3.1)
Clear error message when branch limit reached (TM §3.3)
Retry creates new message with correct parentMessageId and branchIndex

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Enhanced signature: submit(threadId, parentMessageId, prompt, branchIndex?) validates branch limit (max 2 siblings), auto-assigns branchIndex, creates user message with tree metadata, assembles context, submits to AI service.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: BranchLimitCheck interface: {parentMessageId, existingBranches, canCreateBranch, errorMessage}. Enforces maximum 2 retry branches per message.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: User clicks Retry → Edit prompt → Validate branch limit (max 2) → Create branch with correct parentMessageId and branchIndex → Submit to AI → Update UI

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: parentMessageId: string | null (null for root), branchIndex: number (0=original, 1-2=retries)

artifact.reason: Existing thread service - needs createRetry() method with branch validation
Existing input component - needs retry pre-fill logic
Existing message component - needs Retry button added
Message interface with parentMessageId and branchIndex fields

story-context.constraints: - **Desktop Implementation:** Desktop-only (Electron + Svelte). NOT backend Moku API. - **Branch Limit:** Maximum 9 retry branches per message (branchIndex 0-9). Enforce client-side before API call. - **Error Handling:** Throw error "Maximum retry branches reached (max: 2)" if limit exceeded. Show toast notification. - **Attachment References:** Carried-forward attachments use same fileId (no duplication), new attachments uploaded separately. - **Dependency on E2-S1:** Message tree data model must be implemented first (parentMessageId, branchIndex fields).

interface.name: ThreadService.createRetry()
interface.signature: createRetry(messageId: string, modifiedPrompt: string, attachments: Attachment[]): Promise<Message> - Validates branch limit, creates retry with parentMessageId set to parent of original message, assigns next branchIndex

interface.name: RetryButton.svelte
interface.signature: Component that renders retry button on user messages, triggers retry modal with pre-filled prompt

interface.name: validateBranchLimit()
interface.signature: validateBranchLimit(parentMessageId: string): Promise<boolean> - Returns true if can create branch, false if limit reached

tests.standards: Use vitest for unit tests. Test retry button appears on user messages only. Test branch limit validation: create 2 retries successfully, 3rd throws error. Test prompt pre-fill with original content. Test attachment carry-forward with shared fileId references. Test error toast on branch limit.

test.idea: Unit test: Render user message, verify Retry button visible. Render assistant message, verify no Retry button.
Unit test: Click Retry button, verify input field populated with original prompt text, focused
Unit test: Retry with attachments, verify original attachments shown as chips with remove buttons, can add new attachments
Unit test: Carried-forward attachment uses same fileId (verify no duplication in attachment array)
Integration test: Create 2 retries (branchIndex 0,1,2), attempt 3rd, verify error 'Maximum retry branches reached'
Integration test: Retry creates message with parentMessageId = original's parent, branchIndex = next available index
E2E test: Click Retry, edit prompt, submit, verify new branch appears in UI with modified content
